whatIs.title: What Is Hypothyroidism?
whatIs.desc: Hypothyroidism means your thyroid gland is not making enough thyroid hormone. That slows down many body functions, including metabolism, energy, and temperature control.
whatIs.realLife: In real life, it often feels like:
whatIs.r1: You are tired even after sleep
whatIs.r3: Your hair and skin feel different
whatIs.r4: Your mood feels flat or anxious
whatIs.r5: Your periods can change (for many women)

symptoms.title: Common Symptoms (The Ones People Google in Malaysia)
symptoms.desc: Symptoms can build slowly, so it is easy to blame stress, work, or "getting older." Mayo Clinic lists classic symptoms like fatigue, weight gain, constipation, dry skin, slowed heart rate, and thinning hair.
symptoms.s2: Weight gain or difficulty losing weight
symptoms.s8: Low mood, brain fog, poor focus
symptoms.bottomNote: If you feel several of these together, it is worth doing a thyroid function test.

causes.title: Why It Happens (Causes of Hypothyroidism)
causes.c1Desc: Hashimoto's is an autoimmune condition where the immune system attacks the thyroid, which can lead to hypothyroidism over time.
causes.c2Desc: Some people become hypothyroid after thyroid procedures.
causes.c3Title: 3) Iodine imbalance, certain medications, or pituitary causes
causes.c3Desc: Your doctor checks your history and labs to understand the "why," not just the number.
causes.vsDesc: A big reason people delay testing is they think symptoms are "general." The good news is hypothyroidism is one of the most testable causes of chronic tiredness and weight changes.

diagnosis.title: Diagnosis in Kuala Lumpur: How We Confirm It Properly
diagnosis.testsDesc: Most diagnoses start with blood tests:
diagnosis.testsExplain: NHS explains these blood tests are used to check how much TSH and T4 is in your blood. Many Malaysian hospital pages also highlight thyroid function tests for diagnosis.
diagnosis.alsoNeed2: Other tests to rule out look-alikes (iron deficiency, vitamin levels, diabetes, etc.), depending on symptoms
diagnosis.subTitle: Subclinical Hypothyroidism
diagnosis.subDesc1: This is common and confusing. You may have symptoms, but T4 is still in range.
diagnosis.subDesc2: Malaysia's Clinical Practice Guideline includes an approach where treatment is more likely when TSH is higher (for example ≥10), and a watch-and-repeat approach is often used for milder cases, especially depending on age and symptoms.

treatment.title: Hypothyroidism Treatment in Malaysia: What Actually Works
treatment.mainDesc1: Levothyroxine is the standard treatment for hypothyroidism. Malaysia's Clinical Practice Guideline lists levothyroxine as the recommended mainstay treatment.
treatment.mainDesc2: Pantai also explains that levothyroxine replaces thyroxine, often starting low and increasing gradually, with blood tests used to monitor levels. Gleneagles likewise describes hypothyroidism as typically treated with a daily dose of levothyroxine, adjusted through regular blood tests.
treatment.goalsTitle: What good treatment aims to do
treatment.goal2: normalising TSH and thyroid hormone levels
treatment.best1: Take it on an empty stomach
treatment.best3: Or take it at night, 3 to 4 hours after dinner (some people do better with this)
treatment.best4: Avoid taking it close to calcium or iron supplements (separate by about 4 hours)
treatment.monitorDesc1: The American Thyroid Association patient page notes doctors often check blood 6 to 8 weeks after starting or changing the dose.
treatment.monitorDesc2: StatPearls also describes monitoring TSH about 6 to 8 weeks after starting, then later intervals once stable.
treatment.monitorDesc3: NHS notes you may have blood tests quite often at first, but once stable, levels are often checked yearly.

diet.title: Diet, Supplements, and Lifestyle (What Helps Without Hype)
diet.dietDesc1: There is no magic diet that replaces thyroid hormone. If you are truly hypothyroid, medication is the foundation.
diet.dietDesc2: That said, lifestyle can make symptoms easier:
diet.d3: Focus on protein, fibre, and steady meals (helps energy swings)
diet.d4: Treat constipation with water, fibre, and movement
diet.suppDesc: Some supplements can interfere with absorption or blood tests. Always tell your doctor what you take, especially biotin, iron, calcium, and "metabolism boosters."

cost.title: Cost of Hypothyroidism Testing and Treatment in Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur)
cost.c1Desc: A Columbia Asia thyroid screening package is listed at RM100 (includes TSH and some minerals).
cost.c2Desc: Some health screening packages at major hospitals include thyroid tests as part of broader panels (prices vary widely, for example RM799 in one Sunway package listing).
cost.c3Desc: Malaysian online pharmacy listings show levothyroxine brands such as Euthyrox with listed prices (example listings include RM16.50 for a 100 mcg strip on one platform).
cost.bottomNote1: Your monthly cost depends on: dosage, brand vs generic, follow-up testing schedule
cost.bottomNote2: The safest way to budget is to ask for: consult fee + lab range + follow-up plan.

competitor.nexus1: Explain the "how to take levothyroxine" rules clearly (this is where many patients struggle)

faq.q1: What is the best treatment for hypothyroidism?
faq.a1: For most people, the standard treatment is thyroid hormone replacement with levothyroxine, adjusted based on blood tests.
faq.a2: It depends on the cause. Many cases (like Hashimoto's) are long-term and managed with ongoing medication rather than 'cured'.
faq.q3: How do I know if I have hypothyroidism or just stress?
faq.a3: A thyroid function test (TSH and Free T4) helps separate hypothyroidism from other causes of fatigue and weight changes.
faq.q4: How long does levothyroxine take to work?
faq.a4: You may need 4 to 8 weeks to start feeling better.
faq.q5: What should I avoid when taking levothyroxine?
faq.a5: Avoid taking it with food and avoid taking it close to calcium or iron supplements. Many sources recommend taking it on an empty stomach and spacing iron or calcium by several hours.
faq.q6: Can I take levothyroxine at night?
faq.a6: Yes, for many people it can be taken at bedtime as long as it is several hours after food. Malaysia's guideline notes bedtime dosing can be as efficacious as morning dosing, and timing should support adherence.
faq.q7: How often should I test TSH after starting treatment?
faq.a7: ATA guidance for patients notes blood checks around 6 to 8 weeks after starting or changing dose.
faq.a8: Hypothyroidism can slow metabolism and contribute to weight gain.
faq.a9: Thinning hair is listed among common symptoms of hypothyroidism.
faq.q10: Is Hashimoto's the same as hypothyroidism?
faq.a10: Hashimoto's is an autoimmune disease that commonly causes hypothyroidism, but hypothyroidism can have other causes too.
faq.a11: NHS states levothyroxine is safe in pregnancy, and dose may need to increase with regular testing.
faq.q12: How much does a thyroid test cost in Kuala Lumpur?
faq.a12: Some providers list basic thyroid screening packages from around RM100, but prices vary depending on what's included.
